heading: Selection of priority countries and regions
label: 9804 Selection of priority countries and regions

children.label: A
children.text: the national security interests of the United States;

children.label: i
children.text: ranking on recognized global fragility lists, such as the Organization for Economic Co-operation and Development States of Fragility report, the Fund for Peace Fragile States Index, the World Bank Harmonized List of Fragile Situations, the Institute for Economics and Peace Global Peace Index, and the Holocaust Museum Early Warning Project Risk Assessment;

children.label: ii
children.text: ranking on select United States Government conflict and atrocity early warning watch lists;

children.label: iii
children.text: levels of violence, including violence committed by armed groups, state actors, and violent extremist organizations, gender-based violence, and violence against children and youth; and

children.label: iv
children.text: vulnerability to rising sea levels, flooding, drought, wildfires, desertification, deforestation, food insecurity, and human displacement; and

children.label: I
children.text: improving inclusive, transparent, and accountable power structures, including effective, legitimate, and resilient national and sub-national institutions; and

children.label: II
children.text: ensuring strong foundations for human rights, rule of law, and equal access to justice; and

children.label: ii
children.text: the likelihood that United States assistance under the Global Fragility Strategy would measurably help to reduce fragility, prevent the spread of extremism and violence, and stabilize conflict-affected areas in each such country or region; and

in a manner that ensures that not fewer than five countries or regions are selected, including not fewer than two in which the priority will be preventing violent conflict and fragility, rather than stabilizing ongoing conflicts.

children.heading: Consultation with Congress
children.label: b Consultation with Congress
children.text: Prior to finalization of the selection of priority countries and regions under subsection (a), representatives from the Department of State, USAID, the Department of Defense, and other relevant Federal departments and agencies, as necessary and appropriate, shall brief the appropriate congressional committees on the countries and regions being considered and shall consider congressional input on such prioritization.
